edge_hypothesis: The RSI pullback template has a fragile ACCEPT (PF=1.21, DD=9.2%, 38 trades on ETH/4h). The fragility stems from low trade count — 38 trades is barely above statistical significance. This spec attacks both weaknesses: (1) cross-timeframe validation on ETH/1h to increase signal frequency, and (2) relaxed RSI thresholds to capture more pullback events. The core RSI pullback concept is sound: in a confirmed uptrend (close > EMA_200, EMA_50 slope rising), buy when RSI dips — these dips represent temporary profit-taking in a continuing trend. The current rsi_long_max=40 is aggressively tight, requiring RSI to drop to oversold territory during an uptrend, which is rare. Relaxing to 45 captures shallower pullbacks that still represent buying opportunities. On 1h timeframe, RSI oscillations are more frequent, so the same strategy should generate 3-5x more signals. The advisory confirmed that RSI deep-dip (RSI <= 30) is dead on 1h because it's too restrictive — this spec uses moderate pullback levels (40-45), NOT deep-dip. Additionally, this spec applies the extreme R:R principle to RSI pullback — the current ACCEPT uses ~2:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 3.0x TP). By pushing to 4:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 6.0x TP), we test whether pullback entries in trending markets catch large enough continuation moves. Regime assumption: trending — RSI pullback is a trend-continuation strategy. Close > EMA_200 is already a trend gate. Invalidation: (1) if 0 trades on 1h at rsi_long_max=45, the combination of trend filter + RSI threshold is still too restrictive on faster timeframes; (2) if PF < 1.0 with relaxed RSI, the shallower pullbacks are noise, not signal.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_moderate
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with relaxed RSI threshold (rsi_long_max=45) and moderate R:R (2.7:1 at 1.5x stop / 4.0x TP). This is the cross-timeframe validation variant. The 4h ACCEPT uses rsi_long_max=40; relaxing to 45 captures shallower pullbacks that are more frequent on 1h. The 4.0x TP is wider than the 4h ACCEPT (~3.0x) to compensate for fee drag on the faster timeframe. Success criteria: PF > 1.0 with 50+ trades validates the RSI pullback edge across timeframes.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
entry_short: EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (4.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 2.7:1. Needs 27% win rate to break even after fees.
Hypothesis: relaxed RSI threshold (45 vs 40) increases trade count 2-3x on 1h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 4.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend gate + EMA_50 slope confirmation
RSI_14 <= 45 pullback signal — relaxed from 40 to increase 1h trade count
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 4.0x TP — moderate R:R for cross-TF validation

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_tail_harvest
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with standard RSI threshold (rsi_long_max=40) and extreme R:R (4:1 at 1.5x stop / 6.0x TP). Applies the tail harvester principle to the RSI pullback template. The hypothesis: pullback entries in confirmed trends catch continuation moves that can extend 6+ ATR on 1h. Unlike the moderate variant, this keeps the proven RSI=40 threshold (same as the 4h ACCEPT) but widens TP dramatically. If pullback entries catch trend continuations, the move from pullback-low to trend-extension can be substantial. Breakeven win rate: 20%.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 40 indicates meaningful pullback within uptrend.
entry_short: EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 >= 60 indicates meaningful rally within downtrend.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (6.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 4:1. Needs 20% win rate to break even after fees.
Hypothesis: trend continuation from RSI pullback produces 6+ ATR moves on 1h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 6.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend gate + EMA_50 slope — same as proven 4h ACCEPT
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 6.0x TP — 4:1 R:R tail harvest applied to pullback entries
